THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee advanced a nuisance ordinance update and pushed for a county traffic study, while several other local boards were set to take up elections procedures, school standards, and facility repairs Monday evening.

  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee unanimously forwarded revised Chapter 382 Nuisances ordinance language to the Village Board for approval after a July 6 public hearing.
  • The committee also sent a consensus recommendation to Marathon County to conduct a traffic study at County Road XX and Terabonding Drive, with the existing four-way stop staying in place.
  • Staff were directed to research costs and grant options for a bike and pedestrian path along Highway X, with a report expected back to the committee in August.
  • The Kronenwetter Village Board is set to consider proposed revisions to Chapter 41 Elections, ballot drop box installation, and oak wilt regulations at its Monday meeting.
  • The Wausau School Board is set to act on adoption of Wisconsin Academic Standards, WIAA membership renewals for Wausau East, Wausau West, and WAVE, and an EFIS wall repair at South Mountain Elementary.

Coming up: Wausau's Common Council meets Tuesday at 6:30 p.m., preceded by Finance Committee at 5:15 p.m. and Board of Public Works at 10:30 a.m.; Marathon County's Public Safety Committee also convenes Tuesday at 5:00 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
